channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Pothole, 4th March
item.description:
Email received by CSC inbox 03/03/26 17:02 Pothole Lat/Long - 51.4057976, -0.0296099 what3words - claps.giant.tops Nearest address - St Edmonds Presbytery, 20 Village Way, Beckenham, BR3 3NP The ramp drivers are required to drive up to park in Village Way car park (over sainsbury) has a number of deep potholes. Due to the width of the ramp they cannot be avoided. This is not acceptable when people are paying to park and have no other access route. Reference - 5378157 Map link - open Google map Report history - Status: Received 03/03/2026 17:01 The ramp drivers are required to drive up to park in Village Way car park (over sainsbury) has a number of deep potholes. Due to the width of the ramp they cannot be avoided. This is not acceptable when people are paying to park and have no other access route.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 24, Village Way, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pavement trip hazard, 4th March
item.description:
Pavement relayed due to roadworks but has left a gap in between the slabs causing a trip hazard JW
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Pantiles, Bickley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Food waste near/around McDonalds, 4th March
item.description:
On multiple occasions when dog walking there is food waste on the floor - for this particular dog this poses as hazard. My friend's dog is now in the vets for an operation to remove a chicken bone, which has been prevalent on the streets near to the bus stop. I don't believe this is an isolated incident, there needs to be more regular street cleaning or fines presented to those dropping food waste.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 96-98, High Street, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Waste blocking pavement, 4th March
item.description:
This is a continuous waste problem. This is a waste collection point for businesses around here, but passers by (from offices, borough market) dump their personal rubbish there too resulting in missing collections. It smells horrible, it's the perfect environment for pest, during late evening when businesses start placing the bags outside it completely blocks the pavement. Every single day for the past 8 months there is rubbish in that corner.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 4-10, Borough High Street, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The area marked 'X' has not yet been marked or done, 4th March
item.description:
The marked areas continue to be water-logged on my droive way 75 Faringdon Avenue BR2 8BY and sinking down. The area previously marked was done this morning. Thank you. The area marked on my picture continues to be going down and becomes water-logged with any little rain, hence the picture was taken in the rain. As I have explained before, there are young families with buggies plying this way to schools, Elderly wheelchair and scooter users and they have to be either crossing the road to the other side bend round the waterlogged area to get the bus in front of 73/75 Faringdon Avenue. Please attend this request before it leads to accident.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Faringdon Avenue, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lights are short, 4th March
item.description:
The green light is on for few seconds before going red - building up traffic
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2a, Gunter Grove, Brompton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside lights around the ground floor of Chelsfield Point, 4th March
item.description:
About 4 years ago I last reported that the outside lights around the base of Chelsfield Point were on 24/7. They are still on. This is a shocking waste of electricity and money. Can something be done please?
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Penshurst Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
